title: Executive Order 12369—President's Private Sector Survey on Cost Control in the Federal Government

flags.justification: A purely advisory committee of up to 150 private-life members, funded largely by private-sector resources rather than federal appropriations (Sec. 3(e)).
Clear, specific advisory function administered through the Secretary of Commerce.
Multiple related review areas (efficiency, accountability, cost savings) all coherently serve one purpose.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 4(b) sets an explicit termination date: December 31, 1982, unless sooner extended.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
A purely advisory body with independent, private-citizen membership.

The order's own text is narrowly bounded to advisory, time-limited review; its later substantial influence on government-efficiency policy debate came from the resulting report, not from authority claimed in this text.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. This is the 'Grace Commission,' whose eventual report proved highly influential in government-efficiency policy debate for decades — another clean illustration that structural weight and historical significance are distinct measures.
